text:
Diego Park: Quick heads-up — all-hands today at 11:00am PT. Agenda: 1) Exec state & metrics; 2) Dedicated capacity expansion launch callouts; 3) New usage quota policy and migration path; 4) Observability + cost tooling demo; 5) Q&A. Recording and slides will be posted after. Drop short questions here or submit via the form: https://forms.redwood-inference/c/allhands-ask

Ava Chen: excited — will share a 2-slide summary of strategic priorities and hiring asks.

Maya Singh: quick Q — for the quota policy, is this going to be opt-in or enforced automatically for existing hosted customers?

Diego Park: good q. tl;dr: opt-in for tiered billing customers for the first 30 days, then default to soft-enforcement with a 30-day notice. We're piloting with three enterprise customers first. Full migration plan in the follow-up doc.

text:
Sam Nguyen: Quick live-thread from today's all-hands about runtime canaries and targets. Keeping it short so folks can action.
Ava Chen: copying the decisions we agreed on.
Priya Rao: what counts as a canary regression?
Ava Chen: regression = p99 > baseline * 1.12 for 10m OR error-rate >0.5% for 10m OR eval F1 drop >= 2%. Auto-rollback on any trigger.
Kai Patel: baseline scope?
Ava Chen: per-model baseline (last stable release window). ENG-5040 will expose it.
Marta Lopez: published SLOs?
Sam Nguyen: Dedicated SLOs: short p95 <=120ms p99 <=300ms; long (8k+) p95 <=600ms p99 <=1.25s. These are server-inference numbers.
Priya Rao: canary recipe?
Ava Chen: example: `canary-run --model=kernel-v3 --qps=50 --duration=600 --tags=canary-a` then run smoke evals and check dashboards/canaries.
Kai Patel: I'll add dashboard widget (p50/p95/p99, error rate, request size histogram, anomaly band).
Ma...

title: #all-hands: 2345678901
preview:

Ava Chen: I'll paste the leadership answers so folks can act. Short and actionable.
Priya Rao: first question: what's our formal definition of 'regression' for kernel canaries?...
text:
Sam Nguyen: Thanks for joining the follow-up thread from today's all-hands. I want a concise record of the runtime/canary decisions we made.
Ava Chen: I'll paste the leadership answers so folks can act. Short and actionable.
Priya Rao: first question: what's our formal definition of 'regression' for kernel canaries?
Ava Chen: regression = any of: (1) canary p99 latency > baseline p99 * 1.12 for 10m, (2) error rate > 0.4% sustained for 10m, (3) eval prompt F1 drop >= 2% on core prompt set. Any hit triggers auto-rollback and an alert.
Kai Patel: are those baselines per-model or global?
Ava Chen: per-model baseline. Baselines are the last stable release window median; stored in telemetry. ENG-5040 will expose an API to query baselines.
Marta Lopez: follow-up about targets: what are our service targets we should show in docs and to customers?
Sam Nguyen: agreed we need public-facing SLOs....

title: #all-hands: 2325412345
preview:
Sam Nguyen: Thanks everyone for the all-hands — quick follow-up thread for the performance playbook items we promised to clarify.
Ava Chen: putting the leaders' answers here so folks can action.
Priya Rao: can we get the concrete p95/p99 targets for the two critical paths (short chat vs long context spanning 8k+ tok...
text:
Sam Nguyen: Thanks everyone for the all-hands — quick follow-up thread for the performance playbook items we promised to clarify.
Ava Chen: putting the leaders' answers here so folks can action.
Priya Rao: can we get the concrete p95/p99 targets for the two critical paths (short chat vs long context spanning 8k+ tokens)?
Ava Chen: short chat SLO we discussed = p95 < 120ms, p99 < 300ms. Long-context targets are looser: p95 < 600ms, p99 < 1.2s. These are service-level targets for Dedicated tier.
Kai Patel: are those measured server-side (inference-only) or end-to-end (proxy + network)?
Ava Chen: server-side inference only. Console dashboards will show end-to-end soon — tracking work item ENG-4892.
Marta Lopez: follow-up: what's the canary threshold for kernel changes? I want to know when we auto-rollback.
Ava Chen: canary rules: if canary p99 latency increases by >12% vs baseline OR err...

text:
Priya Desai: Thanks everyone for coming to today's all-hands. Posting a live thread for follow-ups on GTM priorities, pipeline health, and partner asks. Drop questions and we'll answer during the next 24h.

Ethan Park: Quick ask — can we clarify the new partner-led deal routing? who owns initial discovery vs. Redwood AE?

Priya Desai: Good question. Short answer: partner-sourced leads => partner engages until SQL, then handoff to Redwood AE for commercial. Partner success stays engaged post-close. Will share playbook link.

Aisha Khan: @Priya can you confirm crediting/commission for partner-influenced renewals? sales ops needs the rule for quotas.

Priya Desai: Yes — renewals credited to the account owner unless contract explicitly routed to partner. Sales Ops to formalize by EOW.

Diego Morales: Anything changing for Dedicated vs Hosted pricing in pipeline motions? Some prospects ask...

text:
Ellen Park: Quick follow-up from the all-hands 11 can leadership clarify the release cadence vs emergency patches?
Ava Chen (CTO): TL;DR: patches = customer-impacting/security -> ship ASAP; minor = biweekly feature train; major = breaking API/infra changes -> monthly window + RFC and cross-team signoff.
Priya Nair: Rollout default: staged canary 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%. Include rollback plan + golden prompts in PR.
Riley (Eng Manager): Oncall role: watch alerts, pause/rollback if thresholds breach. They don't need to ack every expansion unless an alert fires. Owners must post progress in #release-updates.
Ellen Park: What are the auto-rollback triggers we should copy into PRs?
DevOps Bot: ```
Auto-rollback if any of:
- 5xx rate increases > 0.3% absolute for 10m
- error rate > 3x baseline for 5m
- p95 latency > 2x baseline for 10m
```
Samir Patel: Any change to SLO / error-budget poli...

title: #all-hands: 1842509999
preview:
Noah Kim: Quick Q from the all-hands: can we get a concise definition of patch vs minor vs major for the release calendar?
Zara Hughes: Yup. Short answer: patch = urgent bug/security fix; minor = regular feature/iterative work; major = API or infra contract change or anything that needs cross-team migration.
Maya Si...
text:
Noah Kim: Quick Q from the all-hands: can we get a concise definition of patch vs minor vs major for the release calendar?
Zara Hughes: Yup. Short answer: patch = urgent bug/security fix; minor = regular feature/iterative work; major = API or infra contract change or anything that needs cross-team migration.
Maya Singh: Also need clarity on frequency: you mentioned 'cadence' in the talk — is that frozen or guidance?
Ava Chen (CTO): Guidance. Plan is: weekly small patches as needed; a two-week minor cadence (feature train); and a monthly window for bigger platform/major-compat releases. Exceptions allowed with RFC and coordinating owners.
Ben Carter (EngOps): For rollouts we'll use staged expansion with monitoring gates. Suggested default: 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%. Smaller services can skip steps.
Noah Kim: What monitoring gates should be in the PR?
Zara Hughes: Include: baseline error...

title: #all-hands: 1839004567
preview:
Samir Patel: Quick follow-up from today's all-hands — can leadership clarify the new release cadence and what counts as a patch vs minor vs major?
Priya Nair: Great q. TL;DR: patches = hotfix/SEV1 regressions, minor = regular feature releases, major = breaking changes or platform upgrades that need coord with infra....
text:
Samir Patel: Quick follow-up from today's all-hands — can leadership clarify the new release cadence and what counts as a patch vs minor vs major?
Priya Nair: Great q. TL;DR: patches = hotfix/SEV1 regressions, minor = regular feature releases, major = breaking changes or platform upgrades that need coord with infra.
Lena Ortiz: Also want to know where the rollback/autocut threshold is — we had a canary that spiked p95 last week.
Ava Chen (CTO): High level: aiming for a biweekly feature train (every 2 weeks) for non-blocking improvements, monthly non-breaking platform release window for larger work, and immediate patches for incidents.
Ava Chen (CTO): Patch criteria: any customer-facing correctness or reliability regression (SEV1/SEV2) or security fix -> ship as patch ASAP. Minor = backward-compatible features that pass QA. Major = infra or API contract changes, requires RFC + cross-te...

Maya Patel: short answer — there are a few hard deps but several things can be parallelized. i can lis...
text:
Ava Chen: thanks everyone for joining the all-hands — quick follow-up on the roadmap items we touched on. 2 quick asks: can product/eng clarify any hard deps between API GA, Console redesign, and Optimize features?
Maya Patel: short answer — there are a few hard deps but several things can be parallelized. i can list them.
Liam O'Neill: +1 — from eng side we need the new routing infra ready before we push the Console's model-selection UI (otherwise it's just cosmetic).
Sofia Gutierrez: to be explicit: Console redesign MVP depends on the routing infra for model pinning and canary flags. not the entire Dedicated stack.
Raj Singh: routing infra = smart-router + model-variant registry + rollout service. the registry work is biggest blocker (3 sprints).
Ella Park: for UX planning we need a stable API surface for model metadata — ideally a small read-only endpoint. that can land earlier.
Ma...
